title: Sembaruthi Poove Song Lyrics Kadhal Solla Vandhen(1999)
stanzas:
Sembaruthi Poove Song Lyrics Kadhal Solla Vandhen(1999)
#Deva #Hariharan

unai suttri suttri
vandhen ninaivillaiyaa
ennai suthamaaga marandhen
ninaivillaiyaa

poo enna sollum
endru kaatrairyum
katru enna sollum
endru poo ariyum

naan enna solla
vandhen nenjil
enna alli vandhen.
oru nenjam thaan ariyum

ezhudhum kavidhaiyai
evar kannum kaanum munbu
kilithathu ninaivillaiye

unai suttri suttri
vandhen ninaivillaiyaa
ennai suthamaaga marandhen
ninaivillaiyaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Sembaruthi Poove?

The song Sembaruthi Poove expresses deep feelings of love and longing, reflecting the emotions of the protagonist in the movie.

What is the plot context of the movie Kadhal Solla Vandhen?

Kadhal Solla Vandhen revolves around themes of love and relationships, showcasing the struggles and joys of romance.

Who is the singer and music director of the song?

The song is sung by Hariharan and composed by Deva.

When was Sembaruthi Poove released?

The song was released on December 27, 1999.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Sembaruthi Poove, the protagonist expresses his deep affection and yearning for his beloved, encapsulating the essence of romantic love. The song's soothing melody, combined with heartfelt lyrics by Pa Vijay, evokes a sense of nostalgia and longing. It plays a pivotal role in the movie Kadhal Solla Vandhen, highlighting the emotional turmoil of love. The enchanting vocals of Hariharan further elevate the song, making it a timeless classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Sembaruthi Poove from the movie Kadhal Solla Vandhen is a mesmerizing romantic ballad that captures the essence of love and longing. With Hariharan's soulful vocals and Deva's enchanting composition, this song transports listeners to a world of heartfelt emotions. Experience the magic of this classic as it beautifully intertwines poetic lyrics with a captivating melody.
stanzaMeanings: Oh flower of Sembaruthi, oh flower of Sembaruthi, my heart has gone, do you not remember?
Without knowing, you came into my dreams and settled in my heart, do you not remember?
I came around you, do you not remember? I completely forgot myself, do you not remember?
I am thinking of that without saying it. I am suffering without saying it.
Oh flower of Sembaruthi, oh flower of Sembaruthi, my heart has gone, do you not remember?
What does the flower say? The wind asks what does the flower say?
I came to say something, what did I come to say in my heart? Only one heart knows.
What does the sky say? The clouds do not know what it is saying to you.
Why does the flower bloom? The moon does not know why it blooms for you.
I do not know if it is good or bad. I do not understand if it is a bird or a cage.
I am thinking of that without saying it. I am suffering without saying it.
Oh flower of Sembaruthi.
In the window, the moonlight shines, but I do not remember the fight.
The tree has grown, but it has not spoken in my heart, I do not remember.
The book that is in my hand has not written anything anywhere, I do not remember.
The poem that is written has not been seen by anyone, I do not remember.
In the night, there are no dreams, is there no dream? I do not remember.
I am thinking of that without saying it. I am suffering without saying it.
Oh flower of Sembaruthi, oh flower of Sembaruthi, my heart has gone, do you not remember?
Without knowing, you came into my dreams and settled in my heart, do you not remember?
I came around you, do you not remember? I completely forgot myself, do you not remember?
I am thinking of that without saying it. I am suffering without saying it.
